violations.text: Soup and rice were observed held at 109-132 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food items were properly reheated for hot holding at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/15/2025

Steak was observed held at 130 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC reheated food at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/20/2024
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. New Violation. Correct By: 11/20/2024

Establishment was observed selling bottled juice (made in house) without prior approval. Establishment is not permitted to bottle juice at this time. PIC voluntarily disposed of product. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/20/2024

Thermometers are lacking in the refrigeration unit(s). Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 12/04/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the prep units have an accumulation of food residue and debris. New Violation. Correct By: 12/04/2024

Recently Prepped Beef and Chicken held in hot holding equipment were observed held at 122 to 124 degrees Fahrenheit (See Temperatures). Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food items were set for proper reheating at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/15/2023

At the time of inspection, hot holding equipment (Alto Shaam and Hot Hold Steam Unit) were not adjusted/set properly to maintain Proper hot holding temperatures of 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above. Units were adjusted to higher heat settings at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/15/2023

The following food items were observed held in hot holding equipment below 135 degrees Fahrenheit... - Farro in the front steam unit observed at 120 degrees Fahrenheit - Quinoa in the front steam unit observed at 95 degrees Fahrenheit - Chicken in the back hot hold unit observed at 115 degrees Fahrenheit - Beef in the back hot hold unit observed at 120 degrees Fahrenheit - Breaded Chicken in the back hot hold unit observed at 116 degrees Fahrenheit Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food items were set for proper reheating at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2023
The facility is lacking thermo-labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the high temp dish machine. New Violation. Correct By: 08/04/2023
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the Chlorine and the Quaternary Ammonium Tablets sanitizing solution for the 3 Bay Sink. New Violation. Correct By: 08/04/2023
